comment.text: This translation key is used for the text "Remove" in buttons and dialogs to provide a clear and consistent label for removing or deleting content. It appears on buttons such as "Change User Type Modal Submit", "Delete File Modal Submit", and labels for a list of options in a dialog, helping users understand the action being performed.

usage.context:
</Text>
 </ModalDialog.Body>
 <ModalDialog.Footer>
 <Button
 id="change-user-type-modal_submit"
 label={t("Common:Remove")}
 size={ButtonSize.normal}
 scale
 primary
 onClick={onRemove}
 isLoading={isRequestRunning}

</ModalDialog.Body>
 <ModalDialog.Footer>
 <Button
 id="delete-file-modal_submit"
 key="OKButton"
 label={t("Common:Remove")}
 size={ButtonSize.normal}
 primary
 scale
 onClick={onDelete}
 isLoading={isLoading}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Azerbaijani translation 'Silin' is an imperative command meaning 'Be deleted' or 'Delete yourselves' (plural/formal), which is grammatically awkward for a UI button. For a standard action button like 'Remove', 'Sil' (singular imperative) or 'Silin' (if intended as a command to the system, but usually 'Sil' is preferred for buttons) is used, but 'Silin' specifically sounds like a command directed at people. More importantly, 'Silin' is often used as 'Erase/Delete', but in the context of 'Remove' (as in removing a user or an item from a list), 'Sil' or 'Çıxar' is more appropriate. However, the most critical issue is that 'Silin' is the passive/reflexive form 'to be erased', whereas the button is an active command.

The English word 'Remove' is an imperative verb (an action), whereas the Finnish translation 'Tiedostot poistettu' means 'Files deleted' (a past participle/state). This changes the meaning from an action button to a status message.
